architecture.summary: G-PlanET is a planning system where a language model ingests a high-level goal and a structured data table describing objects in a specific environment, then outputs a step-by-step actionable plan for a robotic agent. The system uses table-based environment encoding and iterative decoding to enable grounded plan generation without direct visual perception or physical feedback.

biblio.title: On Grounded Planning for Embodied Tasks with Language Models
biblio.venue: Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence

claims.statement: The use of tables for encoding the environment and an iterative decoding strategy can significantly enhance the LMs' ability in grounded planning
Language models possess commonsense knowledge of the physical world but lack the ability to perceive the environment through vision and feedback from the physical environment
This is the first investigation into whether language models have the capacity to generate grounded, executable plans for embodied tasks
